title: Two-level rc checking (transport rc AND server out_rc) — defensible against checking only the outer FFI return code
description: Most wolfHSM NVM and auth operations have two return codes: the outer FFI call rc (was the request sent and a response received?) and an inner server out_rc (did the server successfully process the request?). The code checks both with Error::check() in sequence. A contributor might suggest 'simplifying' to check only rc, or asserting that out_rc == 0 whenever rc == 0. Neither is correct: the wolfHSM protocol permits rc == 0 (transport succeeded) with out_rc \!= 0 (server rejected the operation, e.g. NVM full, access denied). Collapsing to a single check would silently succeed on server-side failures.
